notes.quote: Again, there were there are multiple instances, but, you know, I I would like to take another example. Instead of the one that I just gave you. So all of a sudden, the devices became noncompliant because from the back end, the minimum PIN requirements was escalated from six to eight digits. Right? And then you know, there was a major major impact because mid majorly, most of the devices in the environment had six digit lock screen PIN. So we ins you know, there was there was there were there was a you know, a tide of multiple tickets of the same issue. So first of all, we open up a problem ticket and then we tagged them to tagged all the child tickets to these this parent ticket, the problem ticket that we opened. And then we also happen to raise an emergency change wherein the minimum requirements were rolled back to six And then we gradually rolled out the communication as the, you know, end users to have a eight digit PIN. That was a security requirement. So we could not avoid it, but we could delay it. So we rolled out a communication plan. It was a three weeks plan. Wherein the users would be asked to update their devices to eight digits If not, then there will follow-up emails and Teams calls. One on one touches. Wherein the users were held to row, you know, in move gradually migrate towards eight digit lock screen.
notes.signal: Monitor policy impact and remediate non-compliant device states in collaboration with stakeholders

transcript.text: Describe a time you had to remediate a spike in non-compliant devices.
transcript.speaker: agent

transcript.text: Again, there were there are multiple instances, but, you know, I I would like to take another example. Instead of the one that I just gave you. So all of a sudden, the devices became noncompliant because from the back end, the minimum PIN requirements was escalated from six to eight digits. Right? And then you know, there was a major major impact because mid majorly, most of the devices in the environment had six digit lock screen PIN. So we ins you know, there was there was there were there was a you know, a tide of multiple tickets of the same issue. So first of all, we open up a problem ticket and then we tagged them to tagged all the child tickets to these this parent ticket, the problem ticket that we opened. And then we also happen to raise an emergency change wherein the minimum requirements were rolled back to six And then we gradually rolled out the communication as the, you know, end users to have a eight digit PIN. That was a security requirement. So we could not avoid it, but we could delay it. So we rolled out a communication plan. It was a three weeks plan. Wherein the users would be asked to update their devices to eight digits If not, then there will follow-up emails and Teams calls. One on one touches. Wherein the users were held to row, you know, in move gradually migrate towards eight digit lock screen.

And then we I went to the the Intune portal and checked for the noncompliance category, it showed that all the device are majorly noncompliant because of the minimum requirements of the lock screen.
